---
title: "Star Schema Design"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Star Schema Design

## Overview

This page documents the star schema created from the cleaned Lightcast job postings dataset. The prepared tables were created in `data_cleaning.qmd` and saved in the `data/prepared/` folder.

The schema uses `job_postings` as the central fact table. The dimension tables describe companies, industries, locations, occupations, and skills. Because each job posting can have many skills, the schema also includes a bridge table called `job_skill_matches`.

## 1. Start Spark Session and Load Prepared Tables

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
import pandas as pd

spark = (
    SparkSession.builder
    .config("spark.driver.host", "localhost")
    .appName("StarSchemaDesign")
    .getOrCreate()
)

spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("ERROR")

def style_table(df, format_dict=None):

    styled = df.style

    if format_dict is not None:

        styled = styled.format(format_dict)

    styled = styled.set_properties(**{

        "text-align": "left",

        "white-space": "normal",

        "border": "1px solid #ddd",

        "padding": "6px"

    }).set_table_styles([

        {"selector": "th", "props": [

            ("background-color", "#f6f8fa"),

            ("color", "#24324b"),

            ("font-weight", "bold"),

            ("border", "1px solid #ddd"),

            ("padding", "8px")

        ]},

        {"selector": "td", "props": [

            ("border", "1px solid #ddd"),

            ("padding", "6px")

        ]},

        {"selector": "table", "props": [

            ("border-collapse", "collapse"),

            ("width", "100%")

        ]}

    ])

    return styled

job_postings = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/job_postings.parquet")

companies = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/companies.parquet")

industries = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/industries.parquet")

locations = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/locations.parquet")

occupations = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/occupations.parquet")

skills = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/skills.parquet")

job_skill_matches = spark.read.parquet("data/prepared/job_skill_matches.parquet")

job_postings.createOrReplaceTempView("fact_job_postings")

companies.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_companies")

industries.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_industries")

locations.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_locations")

occupations.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_occupations")

skills.createOrReplaceTempView("dim_skills")

job_skill_matches.createOrReplaceTempView("fact_job_skill_matches")

```

## 2. Star Schema Tables

```{python}

#| echo: true

schema_summary = pd.DataFrame([

    {

        "table_type": "Fact Table",

        "table_name": "Fact_JobPostings",

        "dataframe": "job_postings",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/job_postings.parquet",

        "description": "Central job posting table. Each row represents one unique job posting."

    },

    {

        "table_type": "Bridge Fact Table",

        "table_name": "Fact_JobSkillMatches",

        "dataframe": "job_skill_matches",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/job_skill_matches.parquet",

        "description": "Bridge table connecting job postings to skills."

    },

    {

        "table_type": "Dimension Table",

        "table_name": "Dim_Companies",

        "dataframe": "companies",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/companies.parquet",

        "description": "Company and employer information."

    },

    {

        "table_type": "Dimension Table",

        "table_name": "Dim_Industries",

        "dataframe": "industries",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/industries.parquet",

        "description": "NAICS industry code and industry name."

    },

    {

        "table_type": "Dimension Table",

        "table_name": "Dim_Locations",

        "dataframe": "locations",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/locations.parquet",

        "description": "City, state, county, and MSA location information."

    },

    {

        "table_type": "Dimension Table",

        "table_name": "Dim_Occupations",

        "dataframe": "occupations",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/occupations.parquet",

        "description": "SOC and Lightcast occupation information."

    },

    {

        "table_type": "Dimension Table",

        "table_name": "Dim_Skills",

        "dataframe": "skills",

        "saved_file": "data/prepared/skills.parquet",

        "description": "Skill name and skill type."

    }

])

display(style_table(schema_summary))

```

## 3. Row Counts

```{python}

#| echo: true

row_counts = pd.DataFrame([

    {"table_name": "Fact_JobPostings", "row_count": job_postings.count()},

    {"table_name": "Fact_JobSkillMatches", "row_count": job_skill_matches.count()},

    {"table_name": "Dim_Companies", "row_count": companies.count()},

    {"table_name": "Dim_Industries", "row_count": industries.count()},

    {"table_name": "Dim_Locations", "row_count": locations.count()},

    {"table_name": "Dim_Occupations", "row_count": occupations.count()},

    {"table_name": "Dim_Skills", "row_count": skills.count()}

])

display(style_table(row_counts, {

    "row_count": "{:,.0f}"

}))

```

## 4. Fact Table: Fact_JobPostings

`Fact_JobPostings` is the central table in the schema. Each row represents one job posting. It stores salary, experience requirements, posting dates, and foreign keys that connect to dimension tables.

```{python}

#| echo: true

fact_job_postings_columns = pd.DataFrame([

    {"column_name": "id", "role": "Primary Key", "description": "Unique job posting ID."},

    {"column_name": "title_clean", "role": "Descriptive Attribute", "description": "Cleaned job title."},

    {"column_name": "company_id", "role": "Foreign Key", "description": "Connects to Dim_Companies."},

    {"column_name": "industry_id", "role": "Foreign Key", "description": "Connects to Dim_Industries."},

    {"column_name": "occupation_id", "role": "Foreign Key", "description": "Connects to Dim_Occupations."},

    {"column_name": "location_id", "role": "Foreign Key", "description": "Connects to Dim_Locations."},

    {"column_name": "employment_type_name", "role": "Descriptive Attribute", "description": "Employment type."},

    {"column_name": "remote_type_name", "role": "Descriptive Attribute", "description": "Remote-work category."},

    {"column_name": "min_years_experience", "role": "Measure", "description": "Minimum required years of experience."},

    {"column_name": "max_years_experience", "role": "Measure", "description": "Maximum required years of experience."},

    {"column_name": "original_salary", "role": "Measure", "description": "Original salary field."},

    {"column_name": "salary_from", "role": "Measure", "description": "Lower salary bound."},

    {"column_name": "salary_to", "role": "Measure", "description": "Upper salary bound."},

    {"column_name": "observed_analysis_salary", "role": "Measure", "description": "Salary before imputation."},

    {"column_name": "salary", "role": "Measure", "description": "Final salary after qualified industry-median imputation."},

    {"column_name": "salary_imputation_status", "role": "Descriptive Attribute", "description": "Observed, imputed, or missing salary status."},

    {"column_name": "posted_date", "role": "Date Attribute", "description": "Parsed posting date."},

    {"column_name": "posted", "role": "Date Attribute", "description": "Original posted date field."},

    {"column_name": "expired", "role": "Date Attribute", "description": "Expiration date field."},

    {"column_name": "duration", "role": "Measure", "description": "Posting duration."}

])

display(style_table(fact_job_postings_columns))

```

## 5. Dimension Tables

```{python}

#| echo: true

dimension_summary = pd.DataFrame([

    {

        "dimension_table": "Dim_Companies",

        "key": "company_id",

        "main_columns": "company, company_name, company_raw, company_is_staffing"

    },

    {

        "dimension_table": "Dim_Industries",

        "key": "industry_id",

        "main_columns": "naics_2022_6, naics_2022_6_name"

    },

    {

        "dimension_table": "Dim_Locations",

        "key": "location_id",

        "main_columns": "location, city_name, state_name, county_name, msa, msa_name"

    },

    {

        "dimension_table": "Dim_Occupations",

        "key": "occupation_id",

        "main_columns": "soc_5, soc_5_name, specialized_occupation_name, occupation_group_name"

    },

    {

        "dimension_table": "Dim_Skills",

        "key": "skill_id",

        "main_columns": "skill_name, skill_type"

    }

])

display(style_table(dimension_summary))

```

## 6. Bridge Table: Fact_JobSkillMatches

A job posting can contain multiple skills, and the same skill can appear in many job postings. Therefore, the schema uses `Fact_JobSkillMatches` as a bridge table between `Fact_JobPostings` and `Dim_Skills`.

```{python}

#| echo: true

bridge_columns = pd.DataFrame([

    {"column_name": "job_id", "role": "Foreign Key", "description": "Connects to Fact_JobPostings.id."},

    {"column_name": "skill_id", "role": "Foreign Key", "description": "Connects to Dim_Skills.skill_id."},

    {"column_name": "skill_name", "role": "Descriptive Attribute", "description": "Skill name."},

    {"column_name": "skill_type", "role": "Descriptive Attribute", "description": "Skill category such as common, specialized, software, or certification."}

])

display(style_table(bridge_columns))

```

## 7. Relationship Summary

```{python}

#| echo: true

relationship_summary = pd.DataFrame([

    {"from_table": "Fact_JobPostings", "from_key": "company_id", "to_table": "Dim_Companies", "to_key": "company_id"},

    {"from_table": "Fact_JobPostings", "from_key": "industry_id", "to_table": "Dim_Industries", "to_key": "industry_id"},

    {"from_table": "Fact_JobPostings", "from_key": "location_id", "to_table": "Dim_Locations", "to_key": "location_id"},

    {"from_table": "Fact_JobPostings", "from_key": "occupation_id", "to_table": "Dim_Occupations", "to_key": "occupation_id"},

    {"from_table": "Fact_JobSkillMatches", "from_key": "job_id", "to_table": "Fact_JobPostings", "to_key": "id"},

    {"from_table": "Fact_JobSkillMatches", "from_key": "skill_id", "to_table": "Dim_Skills", "to_key": "skill_id"}

])

display(style_table(relationship_summary))

```

## 8. Star Schema Diagram

```text

                         Dim_Companies

                              |

                              |

Dim_Industries ---- Fact_JobPostings ---- Dim_Locations

                              |

                              |

                       Dim_Occupations

                              |

                              |

                    Fact_JobSkillMatches

                              |

                              |

                          Dim_Skills

```

## 9. Join Validation Check

```{python}

#| echo: true

join_validation = spark.sql("""

SELECT

    COUNT(*) AS total_job_postings,

    SUM(CASE WHEN company_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_with_company_key,

    SUM(CASE WHEN industry_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_with_industry_key,

    SUM(CASE WHEN location_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_with_location_key,

    SUM(CASE WHEN occupation_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_with_occupation_key

FROM fact_job_postings

""").toPandas()

display(style_table(join_validation, {

    "total_job_postings": "{:,.0f}",

    "jobs_with_company_key": "{:,.0f}",

    "jobs_with_industry_key": "{:,.0f}",

    "jobs_with_location_key": "{:,.0f}",

    "jobs_with_occupation_key": "{:,.0f}"

}))

```

## 10. Example Analytical Query

This example uses the fact table and location dimension to calculate average salary and job count by state.

```{python}

#| echo: true

salary_by_state = spark.sql("""

SELECT

    l.state_name,

    COUNT(*) AS job_count,

    ROUND(AVG(f.salary), 2) AS average_salary

FROM fact_job_postings f

LEFT JOIN dim_locations l

    ON f.location_id = l.location_id

WHERE f.salary IS NOT NULL

GROUP BY l.state_name

ORDER BY job_count DESC

LIMIT 10

""").toPandas()

display(style_table(salary_by_state, {

    "job_count": "{:,.0f}",

    "average_salary": "${:,.2f}"

}))

```
